themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
A comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistently struck by the legitimacy of the Liège waffle and the quality of small-batch ice cream, with specific flavors like sage salted almond getting named; the recurring friction point is staff handling of the sample process, which has surfaced in multiple reviews as dismissive rather than welcoming.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-two Pittsburgh creators have filmed Treat this quarter, generating over 1.1 million TikTok plays from a shop that has posted nothing to Instagram in 200 days. The top video, from @dipitcafe, cleared 296,900 plays on a single waffle-and-ice-cream clip. Meanwhile, Page's holds the Sweets family lead on the back of social momentum, the exact signal Treat is ceding to creators who have no stake in the business. Closing that gap, not the reviews or the visual catalog, which are already competitive, is what moves this ranking.
tldr_read: Over a million TikTok plays this quarter from 32 outside creators, zero Instagram posts in 200 days. Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream.
tldr_meaning: The TikTok signal is already there, built entirely by other people; the rank climbs when the business begins capturing any share of it for itself.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Third In Ice Cream, Fueled By A City Filming It. The Shop Has Not Filmed Back.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram feed this week
playbook.action: The account has been dark for 200 days despite a 7.65% engagement rate sitting idle; one waffle post reactivates that audience immediately.

playbook.headline: Claim a share of TikTok creator traffic
playbook.action: Thirty-two creators have posted 1.1 million plays without any reciprocal account; a single owned TikTok presence ties that reach back to the business.

playbook.headline: Post a visible sample policy in-store
playbook.action: Two separate reviews describe the same sample-limit conflict; a clear posted policy removes the ambiguity before it costs another five-star review.
